display.purpose: Module page titles and primary screen headings.

headline.purpose: Card heads, modal sections, and major panel titles.

body.purpose: Repeated task, note, finance, travel, and settings content.

label.purpose: Dates, counters, compact metadata, weekdays, and system labels.

shadows.name: system-panel
shadows.purpose: Larger panel token, not yet consistently applied.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: Current governed primary action: ice fill, graphite text, compact height.

components.name: Secondary Button
components.description: Current secondary/ghost action: field surface, soft border, quiet text.

components.name: System Card
components.description: Governed card/panel surface used across dashboard modules.

components.name: Input Field
components.description: Default dark inset field; compact modules often override height to 42px.

components.name: Module Header
components.description: Shared PageHeader pattern used by most modules.

narrative.overview: This document describes the UI as it exists now, before any redesign. Rootine OS is implemented as a Vite/React personal life-tracking app in apps/web, with legacy static HTML/CSS files still present at the repository root. The active app has a fixed app shell, a left desktop sidebar, a mobile bottom navigation bar, authenticated routes, and module pages for Planner, Sport, Diet, Finance, Goals, Office, Travel, Work, Notes, and Settings.
keyCharacteristics: Dense, operational, dark product UI with strong module-specific dashboards.
Graphite/ice direction is clear in rendered output, less clear in CSS ownership.
Shared primitives exist, but module-specific panels still define much of the experience.
Responsive behavior is heavily override-driven rather than token-governed.

rules.name: The Ice Is State Rule
rules.body: Ice-blue currently means primary action, selected item, focus, progress, and current period. It should not become decorative glow.

rules.name: The Semantic Color Rule
rules.body: Green, yellow, and red should remain success/warning/danger. Violet, pink, teal, and hot pink need explicit module roles or should be reduced.

rules.name: The Product Type Rule
rules.body: Typography should serve scanning. Display sizing belongs to page titles and major modal titles, not buttons, labels, dense table rows, or badges.

rules.name: The Tonal Depth Rule
rules.body: Use surface level and border strength first. Shadow should confirm hierarchy, not decorate every panel.

dos: Do document and preserve the current graphite/ice direction before changing visuals.
Do treat apps/web as the active product surface and root-level HTML/CSS as legacy unless explicitly revived.
Do make PageLayout, PageHeader, shared cards, shared controls, and rootine-system.css the future governance layer.
Do standardize page-body padding, module header height, card radius, button height, input height, and subnavigation before polishing individual tabs.
Do keep Settings on the fix list; it visually feels the least integrated with the dashboard modules.
donts: Don't redesign yet; this document is an audit of the current UI state.
Don't make Rootine look like a corporate dashboard, SaaS analytics page, or boardroom KPI wall.
Don't make it feel like Notion, a blank block canvas, or a generic productivity template.
Don't polish one tab by adding more local CSS overrides before the shared raster is stabilized.
Don't add another subnavigation pattern; map new needs to tabs, segmented controls, filter chips, or category rails.
Don't rely on !important as the normal implementation path for new UI.
